channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 4 states head to the polls. And, global conflicts are on the rise, new report says
item.description: Voters in four states head to the polls today for their primaries. Here are the races to watch. And, global conflicts are at their highest level since World War II, data shows.
item.content:encoded:
Voters in four states head to the polls today for their primaries. Here are the races to watch. And, global conflicts are at their highest level since World War II, data shows.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: More Americans survive cancer. But the psychological impacts can persist for years
item.description: Due to advancements in treatment and screening, more Americans are surviving cancer. But many are left with lingering mental health challenges like anxiety and depression.
item.content:encoded:
Due to advancements in treatment and screening, more Americans are surviving cancer. But many are left with lingering mental health challenges like anxiety and depression.

(Image credit: Natalie Krebs)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Krebs

item.title: The Supreme Court is in its final stretch this term. Here are the major cases left
item.description: The Supreme Court is heading into its crunch time, the part of the year when the justices are racing to finish decisions and dissents in the cases that remain undecided. Here's what's left.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court is heading into its crunch time, the part of the year when the justices are racing to finish decisions and dissents in the cases that remain undecided. Here's what's left.

(Image credit: Tyrone Turner)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Maine's Senate race and much more. Here are the primary contests to watch today
item.description: Republican incumbents are facing tough challenges in Maine and Nevada. In South Carolina, a crowded field of MAGA-devoted Republicans are facing off to be the next governor.
item.content:encoded:
Republican incumbents are facing tough challenges in Maine and Nevada. In South Carolina, a crowded field of MAGA-devoted Republicans are facing off to be the next governor.

(Image credit: John Locher)
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: GLP-1s appear to protect against cancer. Researchers are trying to figure out how
item.description: Recent studies suggest the weight loss and diabetes drugs may prevent cancer and slow its progression. While weight loss is known to curb cancer risks, GLP-1s may act on other brain and metabolic pathways to prevent cancer.
item.content:encoded:
Recent studies suggest the weight loss and diabetes drugs may prevent cancer and slow its progression. While weight loss is known to curb cancer risks, GLP-1s may act on other brain and metabolic pathways to prevent cancer.

(Image credit: Michael Siluk)
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now, voters in four states head to the polls Tuesday for primaries, Trump makes baseless claims about election fraud in California.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran agree to stop strikes for now, voters in four states head to the polls Tuesday for primaries, Trump makes baseless claims about election fraud in California.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Pope Leo calls AI firms a new form of colonialism, echoing tech critics
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Conflicts around the globe hit highest levels since World War II, new report says
item.description: The new report by the Uppsala Conflict Data Program said the massacres in El Fasher pushed one-sided violence in Africa to its highest levels since the Rwandan genocide in 1994.
item.content:encoded: The new report by the Uppsala Conflict Data Program said the massacres in El Fasher pushed one-sided violence in Africa to its highest levels since the Rwandan genocide in 1994.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Akinwotu

item.title: Conflicts are on the rise globally, at the highest level since WWII, data shows
item.description: Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Uppsala Conflict Data Program. Fatalities were the highest on record since 1994, with approximately 244,600 people killed in conflict in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
Global conflicts surged to the highest number tallied by Uppsala Conflict Data Program. Fatalities were the highest on record since 1994, with approximately 244,600 people killed in conflict in 2025.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools
item.description: Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.
item.content:encoded:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
